accurate.why: All claims match the bill text (figures, section, statute) and accurate context about the SUPPORT Act and the youth prevention and recovery initiative is verified.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the committee referrals and program description are accurate context consistent with the bill and record.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill reauthorizes/amends the youth prevention and recovery initiative's appropriations under the SUPPORT Act.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies the amendment to Section 7102(c)(9) of the SUPPORT for Patients and Communities Act.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions or conditions, only an appropriations reauthorization.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the fiscal year 2026 through 2030 timeframe of the authorized appropriations.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the bill's sole operative effect of reauthorizing appropriations at the specified annual levels.

objective_tone.why: Summary is neutral and non-partisan with no editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized, internally consistent, and clearly written.

concise.why: Largely concise, listing each figure without excessive padding.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the SUPPORT for Patients and Communities Act, Section 7102(c)(9), and the two House committees of referral.

correct_figures.why: All dollar amounts and fiscal years ($10M FY2026 through $15M FY2030) match the bill text exactly.
